Title: Cryptic isoprene emission of soybeans
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: terpene synthase terminal step of hemiterpene/isoprene formation
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TPS-b terpene synthase family; DDXXD motif; conserved ISPS residues F,S,F,N
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes isoprene formation in vitro and contributes to wound/burn-induced cryptic isoprene emission in soybean leaves
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmTPS8 is an intact soybean ISPS candidate with conserved diagnostic ISPS residues and DDXXD motif. 2. Recombinant GmTPS8 expressed in E. coli converted DMADP to isoprene in vitro. 3. GmTPS8 showed low catalytic efficiency and substrate inhibition above 2 mM DMADP, consistent with cryptic rather than constitutive emission. 4. GmTPS8 transcript abundance increased after wounding, accompanying transient isoprene emission from soybean leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In silico sequence/structure analysis; recombinant expression in E. coli; purified enzyme assay with DMADP; kinetic analysis; qRT-PCR after wounding; leaf isoprene profiling by fast isoprene sensor and GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Chloroplast, inferred for plastidial terpene synthase/isoprene biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Leaf; mature reproductive-stage leaves; upregulated after wounding
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf; mature reproductive-stage leaves; upregulated after wounding
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Mechanical wounding; burning; elevated CO2; short-term and long-term heat stress
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; terminal determinant with low catalytic efficiency/high substrate requirement contributing to cryptic emission
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Stress-associated volatile implicated in heat tolerance, oxidative stress responses, defense signaling, and atmospheric chemistry
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf emission from undamaged portions of wounded/burned leaves
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: IPTG-inducible promoter in E. coli for recombinant expression
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GmTPS8 had approximately 16-fold lower kcat than EgISPS and higher K1/2, indicating lower DMADP affinity/efficiency; activity declined beyond 2 mM DMADP due to substrate inhibition; although expression was higher than GmTPS23, catalytic activity was lower
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to modulate cryptic isoprene emission and stress resilience in soybean; engineering use should consider atmospheric/ozone tradeoffs and carbon cost
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced isoprene may impose carbon and energy costs and contribute to ozone pollution; under stress emission is linked with reduced photosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: terpene synthase terminal step of hemiterpene/isoprene formation
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TPS-b terpene synthase family; DDXXD motif; conserved ISPS residues F,S,F,N
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes isoprene formation in vitro and is strongly induced by wounding, aligning with transient cryptic isoprene emission in soybean leaves
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmTPS23 is an intact soybean ISPS with canonical diagnostic residues and terpene synthase motif. 2. Recombinant GmTPS23 converted DMADP to isoprene in vitro in a time- and concentration-dependent manner. 3. GmTPS23 showed stronger wound induction than GmTPS8 and greater catalytic activity than GmTPS8, matching its proposed major contribution to wound-induced isoprene emission. 4. GmTPS23 also displayed low catalytic efficiency relative to eucalyptus ISPS and substrate inhibition above 2 mM DMADP.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In silico sequence/structure analysis; recombinant expression in E. coli; purified enzyme assay with DMADP; kinetic analysis; qRT-PCR after wounding; leaf isoprene profiling by fast isoprene sensor and GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Chloroplast, inferred for plastidial terpene synthase/isoprene biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Leaf; mature reproductive-stage leaves; strongly induced after wounding
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf; mature reproductive-stage leaves; strongly induced after wounding
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Mechanical wounding; burning; elevated CO2; short-term and long-term heat stress
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; terminal determinant with low catalytic efficiency/high substrate requirement contributing to cryptic emission
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Stress-associated volatile implicated in heat tolerance, oxidative stress responses, defense signaling, and atmospheric chemistry
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf emission from undamaged portions of wounded/burned leaves
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: IPTG-inducible promoter in E. coli for recombinant expression
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GmTPS23 had about 4-fold lower kcat than EgISPS and higher K1/2; likely more important than GmTPS8 for wound-induced soybean isoprene because it is more inducible and more catalytically active
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target for engineering inducible isoprene emission and stress adaptation in soybean, but deployment should consider carbon cost and atmospheric effects
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced isoprene may impose carbon and energy costs and contribute to ozone pollution; stress-associated emission coincides with reduced photosynthesis
